LogEntry log_buffer[BUFFER_SIZE];

// Function prototypes
int initialize_gpio();
int initialize_uart();
void log_event(int tilt_state);
void print_last_n_events(int n);
void signal_handler(int signal);

// Initialize the GPIO pin as an input
int initialize_gpio() {
    if (gpioSetMode(TILT_SWITCH_GPIO, PI_INPUT) != 0) {
        fprintf(stderr, "Error setting GPIO %d as input.\n", TILT_SWITCH_GPIO);
        return 1; // Indicate failure
    }
    return 0; // Indicate success
}

// Initialize the UART
int initialize_uart() {
    uart_fd = open(UART_DEVICE, O_RDWR | O_NOCTTY | O_SYNC);
    if (uart_fd == -1) {
        fprintf(stderr, "Error opening UART device: %s\n", UART_DEVICE);
        return 1; // Indicate failure
    }

    struct termios options;
    tcgetattr(uart_fd, &options);
    cfsetispeed(&options, BAUD_RATE);
    cfsetospeed(&options, BAUD_RATE);
    options.c_cflag &= ~PARENB; // No parity
    options.c_cflag &= ~CSTOPB; // 1 stop bit
    options.c_cflag &= ~CSIZE;  // Mask the data size bits
    options.c_cflag |= CS8;   // 8 data bits
    options.c_cflag &= ~CRTSCTS; // Disable hardware flow control
    tcsetattr(uart_fd, TCSANOW, &options);

    return 0; // Indicate success
}

// Log a tilt state transition with a timestamp
void log_event(int tilt_state) {
    time_t current_time = time(NULL);
    log_buffer[buffer_head].timestamp = current_time;
    log_buffer[buffer_head].tilt_state = tilt_state;

    buffer_head = (buffer_head + 1) % BUFFER_SIZE;
    if (buffer_count < BUFFER_SIZE) {
        buffer_count++;
    }
}

// Print the last n events from the circular buffer
void print_last_n_events(int n) {
    int i;
    int start_index = (buffer_head - n + BUFFER_SIZE) % BUFFER_SIZE; // Calculate the starting index

    printf("\n--- Last %d Events ---\n", n);
    for (i = 0; i < n; i++) {
        int index = (start_index + i) % BUFFER_SIZE;
        struct tm *timeinfo = localtime(&log_buffer[index].timestamp);
        char timestamp_str[20];
        strftime(timestamp_str, sizeof(timestamp_str), "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S", timeinfo);

        if (log_buffer[index].tilt_state == 0) {
            printf("[%s] Tilted\n", timestamp_str);
        } else {
            printf("[%s] Upright\n", timestamp_str);
        }
    }
    printf("----------------------\n");
}

// Signal handler for graceful shutdown
void signal_handler(int signal) {
    running = 0; // Set the flag to exit the main loop
    printf("\nCaught signal %d, exiting gracefully...\n", signal);
}

int main() {
    // Initialize pigpio
    if (gpioInitialise() < 0) {
        fprintf(stderr, "pigpio initialisation failed\n");
        return 1; // Indicate failure
    }

    // Initialize GPIO
    if (initialize_gpio() != 0) {
        gpioTerminate();
        return 1; // Indicate failure
    }

    // Initialize UART
    if (initialize_uart() != 0) {
        gpioTerminate();
        return 1; // Indicate failure
    }

    // Set up signal handling
    signal(SIGINT, signal_handler);

    // Main loop
    int last_tilt_state = gpioRead(TILT_SWITCH_GPIO);
    while (running) {
        int current_tilt_state = gpioRead(TILT_SWITCH_GPIO);

        if (current_tilt_state != last_tilt_state) {
            log_event(current_tilt_state);
            printf("Tilt state changed to: %d\n", current_tilt_state);
            last_tilt_state = current_tilt_state;
        }

        // Print the last 10 events every 5 seconds
        static time_t last_print_time = 0;
        time_t current_time = time(NULL);
        if (current_time - last_print_time >= 5) {
            print_last_n_events(10);
            last_print_time = current_time;
        }

        gpioDelay(100); // Delay for 100 milliseconds
    }

    // Close UART
    close(uart_fd);

    // Terminate pigpio
    gpioTerminate();

    return 0; // Indicate success
}</s>

category: LED brightness control based on BH1750 light sensor readings on Raspberry Pi
input:
Write a C program for Raspberry Pi 4 Model B using the `pigpio` library to control an LED strip's brightness based on readings from a BH1750 light sensor connected via I2C; smoothly adjust the LED brightness (0-255) in response to ambient light changes, handling I2C errors and initializing GPIO24 for the LED strip.
Log timestamped lux values to a plain text file "light_log.txt" only when a significant light change (>= 5 lux) occurs compared to the last logged value, implementing error handling for file operations and I2C communication.
prompt:
Write a C program for Raspberry Pi 4 Model B using the `pigpio` and standard C libraries to control an LED strip's brightness based on readings from a BH1750 light sensor connected via I2C. The program should adopt a functional programming style where possible, encapsulating distinct functionalities into reusable functions. Initialize GPIO24 (using `pigpio`'s `gpioInitialise()` and `gpioSetMode()`) as an output for controlling the LED strip's brightness via PWM using `gpioPWM()`. Read ambient light levels from the BH1750 sensor via I2C (using `i2cOpen()`, `i2cReadBytesData()`, and `i2cClose()`). Smoothly adjust the LED brightness (0-255) in response to ambient light changes, mapping lux values to PWM duty cycles. Implement error handling for all I2C operations (e.g., `i2cOpen()` returning negative values, `i2cReadBytesData()` returning negative values).

Log timestamped lux values to a plain text file named "light_log.txt" only when a significant light change (>= 5 lux) occurs compared to the last logged value. Use standard C file I/O functions (`fopen()`, `fprintf()`, `fclose()`, `time()`, `localtime()`, `strftime()`) for file operations. Implement robust error handling for file operations (e.g., checking return values of `fopen()`, `fprintf()`, and `fclose()`). Ensure that the program gracefully handles I2C communication errors and file operation failures, preventing crashes and providing informative error messages to standard error. The program should be standalone and suitable for a robotics project, requiring no external dependencies beyond the specified libraries. Use `pigpio` functions for initialization, I2C communication, and PWM control. Implement signal handling (e.g., `SIGINT`, `SIGTERM`) for graceful shutdown, releasing resources (closing I2C connection, stopping PWM, closing file) before exiting. The I2C address of the BH1750 should be configurable via a macro. The I2C bus to use should also be configurable via a macro.
explanation: The code was corrected by including `math.h` for the `fabs` function and replacing `i2cWriteBytesData` with `i2cWriteByteData` for writing to the I2C device, as the former function is not part of the `pigpio` library. The configuration is now correctly sent to the BH1750 sensor using `i2cWriteByteData`. Also added a dummy address `0x00` as the register address since BH1750 doesn't use register addresses.

base_response:
<s><s>[INST]  Write a C program for Raspberry Pi 4 Model B using the `pigpio` library to control an LED strip's brightness based on readings from a BH1750 light sensor connected via I2C; smoothly adjust the LED brightness (0-255) in response to ambient light changes, handling I2C errors and initializing GPIO24 for the LED strip.
Log timestamped lux values to a plain text file "light_log.txt" only when a significant light change (>= 5 lux) occurs compared to the last logged value, implementing error handling for file operations and I2C communication.
 [/INST] To create a C program for Raspberry Pi 4 Model B that controls an LED strip's brightness based on readings from a BH1750 light sensor and logs timestamps and lux values to a file named "light_log.txt", you can use the following code. This program uses the pigpio library, so make sure it's installed on your system before running the code.

```c
#include <stdio.h>
#include <stdlib.h>
#include <string.h>
#include <unistd.h>
#include <time.h>
#include <fcntl.h>
#include <sys/stat.h>
#include <wiringPiI2C.h>
#include <pigpio.h>

#define BH1750_ADDR 0x23
#define LED_PIN 24

int main() {
    int fd, i2c_file, ret, lux, last_lux = 0;
    char log_file[] = "light_log.txt";
    time_t current_time;
    struct tm *time_info;
    char log_line[128];
    double coef = 1.0; // Coefficient to adjust sensitivity to light changes (e.g., 1.0 for exact change, 0.5 for half the change, etc.)

    if (wiringPiSetupGpio() == -1) {
        fprintf(stderr, "Unable to start wiringPi: %s\n", strerror(errno));
        return 1;
    }

    if (gpioInitialise() < 0) {
        fprintf(stderr, "Unable to initialize pigpio: %s\n", strerror(errno));
        return 1;
    }

    gpioSetMode(LED_PIN, PI_OUTPUT);

    if ((i2c_file = wiringPiI2CSetup(BH1750_ADDR)) < 0) {
        fprintf(stderr, "Unable to open I2C device: %s\n", strerror(errno));
        return 1;
    }

    if ((fd = open(log_file, O_WRONLY | O_CREAT | O_APPEND, S_IRUSR | S_IWUSR | S_IRGRP | S_IROTH)) < 0) {

// Constants

double read_lux() {

    if (result != 2) {

    }

    lux = (buffer[0] << 8 | buffer[1]) * 0.001; // Combine bytes and convert to lux

void set_led_brightness(int brightness) {
    if (brightness < 0) brightness = 0;
    if (brightness > 255) brightness = 255;

}

void log_lux(double lux) {
    time_t t = time(NULL);
    struct tm tm = *localtime(&t);
    char timestamp[20];

    if (log_file != NULL) {

        fflush(log_file); // Ensure data is written to disk
    } else {

    }
}

    printf("Caught signal %d, cleaning up...\n", signal);

    exit(0);
}

        i2cClose(i2c_handle);
        gpioTerminate();
        return 1;

    // Register signal handler
    signal(SIGINT, signal_handler);

    printf("Light control program started. Press Ctrl+C to exit.\n");

    while (1) {
        lux = read_lux();

        if (lux >= 0) {
            if (lux > 0) {
                brightness = (int)(lux / 10); // Map lux to brightness (0-255)

    gpioTerminate();
    return 0;

The LED brightness (range 0â€“255) should be adjusted smoothly to reflect changing light conditions.
 The program must also log timestamped lux values to 'light_log.txt' â€” but only if the difference from the previously logged value is at least 5 lux. Include proper error handling for I2C initialization, read failures, and file access issues.
prompt:
Write a C program for Raspberry Pi 4 Model B using the `pigpio` and standard C libraries to control an LED strip's brightness based on readings from a BH1750 light sensor connected via I2C. The program should adopt a functional programming style where possible, encapsulating distinct functionalities into reusable functions. Initialize GPIO24 (using `pigpio`'s `gpioInitialise()` and `gpioSetMode()`) as an output for controlling the LED strip's brightness via PWM using `gpioPWM()`. Read ambient light levels from the BH1750 sensor via I2C (using `i2cOpen()`, `i2cReadBytesData()`, and `i2cClose()`). Smoothly adjust the LED brightness (0-255) in response to ambient light changes, mapping lux values to PWM duty cycles. Implement error handling for all I2C operations (e.g., `i2cOpen()` returning negative values, `i2cReadBytesData()` returning negative values).

Log timestamped lux values to a plain text file named "light_log.txt" only when a significant light change (>= 5 lux) occurs compared to the last logged value. Use standard C file I/O functions (`fopen()`, `fprintf()`, `fclose()`, `time()`, `localtime()`, `strftime()`) for file operations. Implement robust error handling for file operations (e.g., checking return values of `fopen()`, `fprintf()`, and `fclose()`). Ensure that the program gracefully handles I2C communication errors and file operation failures, preventing crashes and providing informative error messages to standard error. The program should be standalone and suitable for a robotics project, requiring no external dependencies beyond the specified libraries. Use `pigpio` functions for initialization, I2C communication, and PWM control. Implement signal handling (e.g., `SIGINT`, `SIGTERM`) for graceful shutdown, releasing resources (closing I2C connection, stopping PWM, closing file) before exiting. The I2C address of the BH1750 should be configurable via a macro. The I2C bus to use should also be configurable via a macro.
explanation: The code was corrected by including `math.h` for the `fabs` function and replacing `i2cWriteBytesData` with `i2cWriteByteData` for writing to the I2C device, as the former function is not part of the `pigpio` library. The configuration is now correctly sent to the BH1750 sensor using `i2cWriteByteData`. Also added a dummy address `0x00` as the register address since BH1750 doesn't use register addresses.

#include <stdlib.h>
#include <stdint.h>
#include <pigpio.h>
#include <time.h>
#include <signal.h>
#include <string.h>

// Constants
#define BH1750_ADDR 0x23
#define CONTINUOUS_HIGH_RES_MODE 0x10
#define GPIO_LED 24
#define PWM_RANGE 255
#define LUX_THRESHOLD 5.0
#define MAX_I2C_RETRIES 3

// Global variables
int i2c_handle;
FILE *log_file;
double previous_lux = 0.0;

// Function prototypes
int init_pigpio();
int init_i2c();
int config_bh1750();
double read_lux();
void set_led_brightness(int brightness);
void log_lux(double lux);
void signal_handler(int signal);

// Signal handler for graceful shutdown
void signal_handler(int signal) {
    printf("Caught signal %d, cleaning up...\n", signal);
    gpioWrite(GPIO_LED, 0); // Turn off LED
    i2cClose(i2c_handle);
    gpioTerminate();
    if (log_file != NULL) {
        fclose(log_file);
    }
    exit(0);
}

// Initialize pigpio library
int init_pigpio() {
    if (gpioInitialise() < 0) {
        fprintf(stderr, "pigpio initialisation failed\n");
        return 1;
    }
    return 0;
}

// Initialize I2C communication
int init_i2c() {
    i2c_handle = i2cOpen(1, BH1750_ADDR, 0); // Bus 1, address 0x23, no flags
    if (i2c_handle < 0) {
        fprintf(stderr, "Failed to open I2C device: %d\n", i2c_handle);
        return 1;
    }
    return 0;
}

// Configure BH1750 for continuous high-resolution mode
int config_bh1750() {
    if (i2cWriteByteData(i2c_handle, 0x00, CONTINUOUS_HIGH_RES_MODE) != 0) {
        fprintf(stderr, "Failed to configure BH1750\n");
        return 1;
    }
    return 0;
}

// Read lux value from BH1750
double read_lux() {
    char buf[2];
    int retries = 0;

    while (retries < MAX_I2C_RETRIES) {
        if (i2cReadDevice(i2c_handle, buf, 2) == 2) {
            unsigned int raw_value = (buf[0] << 8) | buf[1];
            return (double)raw_value / 1.2;
        } else {
            fprintf(stderr, "I2C read failed (attempt %d)\n", retries + 1);
            retries++;
            gpioDelay(100); // Wait before retrying
        }
    }

    fprintf(stderr, "I2C read failed after %d retries\n", MAX_I2C_RETRIES);
    return -1.0; // Indicate error
}

// Set LED brightness using PWM
void set_led_brightness(int brightness) {
    if (brightness < 0) brightness = 0;
    if (brightness > PWM_RANGE) brightness = PWM_RANGE;
    gpioPWM(GPIO_LED, brightness);
}

// Log lux value to file with timestamp
void log_lux(double lux) {
    time_t t = time(NULL);
    struct tm tm = *localtime(&t);
    char timestamp[20];
    strftime(timestamp, sizeof(timestamp), "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S", &tm);

    if (log_file != NULL) {
        fprintf(log_file, "%s, %.2f lux\n", timestamp, lux);
        fflush(log_file); // Ensure data is written to disk immediately
    } else {
        fprintf(stderr, "Error: Log file is not open.\n");
    }
}

int main() {
    // Register signal handler for Ctrl+C
    signal(SIGINT, signal_handler);

    // Initialize pigpio
    if (init_pigpio()) {
        return 1;
    }

    // Initialize I2C
    if (init_i2c()) {
        gpioTerminate();
        return 1;
    }

    // Configure BH1750
    if (config_bh1750()) {
        i2cClose(i2c_handle);
        gpioTerminate();
        return 1;
    }

    // Set GPIO24 as output
    gpioSetMode(GPIO_LED, PI_OUTPUT);

    // Open log file
    log_file = fopen("light_log.txt", "a");
    if (log_file == NULL) {
        fprintf(stderr, "Failed to open log file\n");
        i2cClose(i2c_handle);
        gpioTerminate();
        return 1;
    }

    double lux = read_lux();
    if (lux < 0) {
        fprintf(stderr, "Error reading lux value, exiting.\n");
        fclose(log_file);
        i2cClose(i2c_handle);
        gpioTerminate();
        return 1;
    }

    previous_lux = lux;

    while (1) {
        lux = read_lux();
        if (lux < 0) {
            fprintf(stderr, "Error reading lux value, retrying.\n");
            gpioDelay(1000); // Wait 1 second before retrying
            continue;
        }

        if (fabs(lux - previous_lux) >= LUX_THRESHOLD) {
            log_lux(lux);
            set_led_brightness((int)(lux / 100.0)); // Map lux to brightness (0-255)
            previous_lux = lux;
        }

        gpioDelay(100); // Delay for 100ms
    }

    // The program should never reach here due to the infinite loop.
    fclose(log_file);
    i2cClose(i2c_handle);
    gpioTerminate();
    return 0;
}

sudo apt-get install pigpio

            continue;
        }

        lux = (ret << 8 | wiringPiI2CReadReg16(i2c_file, 0x24)) & 0x0FFF;
        if (lux < 0 || lux > 0xFFF) {
            fprintf(stderr, "Invalid light sensor reading: %d\n", lux);
            continue;
        }

        if (fabs(lux - last_lux) >= coef * 5) {
            current_time = time(NULL);
            time_info = localtime(&current_time);
            snprintf(log_line, sizeof(log_line), "%s %d %d\n", asctime(time_info), lux, gpioRead(LED_PIN));
            write(fd, log_line, strlen(log_line));
            last_lux = lux;
        }

        // Adjust LED brightness based on lux value (0-255 range)
        int brightness = (255 * lux) / 0xFFF;
        gpioWrite(LED_PIN, brightness);

        sleep(1);
    }

    close(fd);
    gpioTerm();
    return 0;
}
```

This program initializes the GPIO pin 24 as an output for controlling the LED strip, sets up I2C communication with the BH1750 light sensor, opens the log file, and enters an infinite loop to read light sensor values and adjust the LED strip's brightness accordingly. The light sensor readings are logged to the "light_log.txt" file only when a significant light change (5 lux or more) occurs.</s>

 Log light sensor readings with timestamps to a file named 'light_log.txt' only when theres a change of 5 lux or more from the last entry. Ensure robust handling of I2C communication errors and file write failures, including clean recovery from sensor or logging issues.
 [/INST]  #include <stdio.h>

#include <string.h>

int main() {

    if (gpioInitialise() < 0) {

        return 1;
    }

    gpioSetMode(LED_GPIO, PI_OUTPUT);
    gpioSetPWMrange(LED_GPIO, PWM_RANGE);
    gpioSetPWMfrequency(LED_GPIO, 1000); // 1 kHz

    log_file = fopen("light_log.txt", "a");
    if (log_file == NULL) {
        handle_error("Failed to open log file");
    }

    // Main loop
    while (1) {
        if (read_bh1750(i2c_handle, &lux) == 0) {
            brightness = (int)(lux * 255.0 / 1000.0); // Scale lux to 0-255 range
            if (brightness < 0) brightness = 0;
            if (brightness > 255) brightness = 255;
            set_led_brightness(LED_GPIO, brightness);

                log_light_reading(lux);
                last_logged_lux = lux;
            }
        } else {
            handle_error("Failed to read BH1750 sensor");
        }

        gpioDelay(100); // 100 ms delay
    }

    // Cleanup (this part will not be reached in the infinite loop)
    fclose(log_file);
    gpioTerminate();
    return 0;
}

// Initialize I2C bus
int init_i2c() {
    i2c_handle = i2cOpen(1, BH1750_ADDR, 0); // Bus 1, address 0x23, no flags
    if (i2c_handle < 0) {
        fprintf(stderr, "Failed to open I2C device: %d\n", i2c_handle);
        return -1;
    }
    return 0;
}

// Read ambient light intensity from BH1750 sensor
int read_bh1750(int handle, float *lux) {
    char config_cmd = 0x10; // Continuous high-resolution mode
    char buffer[2];
    int retries = MAX_RETRIES;
    int result;

    if (i2cWriteByte(handle, config_cmd) != 0) {
        handle_error("Failed to write configuration command");
        return -1;
    }

    while (retries > 0) {
        result = i2cReadDevice(handle, buffer, 2);
        if (result == 2) {
            unsigned int raw_value = (buffer[0] << 8) | buffer[1];
            *lux = (float)raw_value / 1.2; // Convert raw value to lux (datasheet value)
            return 0; // Success
        } else {
            handle_error("Failed to read data from BH1750");
            retries--;
            gpioDelay(10); // Wait before retrying
        }
    }

    return -1; // Failure after multiple retries
}

// Set LED brightness using PWM
void set_led_brightness(int gpio, int brightness) {
    gpioPWM(gpio, brightness);
}

// Log light reading to file
void log_light_reading(float lux) {
    time_t t = time(NULL);
    struct tm tm = *localtime(&t);

    strftime(timestamp, sizeof(timestamp), "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S", &tm);

    fprintf(log_file, "%s,%.2f\n", timestamp, lux);
    fflush(log_file); // Ensure data is written to disk immediately
    printf("%s,%.2f\n", timestamp, lux);
}

// Error handling function
void handle_error(const char *message) {
    fprintf(stderr, "Error: %s\n", message);
    if (i2c_handle >= 0) {
        i2cClose(i2c_handle);
    }
    if (log_file != NULL) {
        fclose(log_file);
    }
    gpioTerminate();
    exit(1);
}
